regions.us-ports: Today, Miami, the primary US cruise homeport, is experiencing clear skies with a SSE wind at 6kt and less than 10% rain probability, which will have no impact on port operations or shore excursions. As we look ahead to the next 24-48 hours, Port Everglades and Port Canaveral can expect overcast skies with SSE winds at 13kt and less than 10% rain probability on Day 1, while on Day 2, Miami and Tampa Bay may see rain showers with SSE winds at 9kt and less than 10% rain probability. Beyond 48 hours, a change in weather is expected, with overcast skies and a 21% rain probability forecast for Miami on Day 3, which could affect shore excursions. The four primary US cruise homeports, including Miami, Port Everglades, Port Canaveral, and Tampa Bay, will continue to experience SSE winds, with the wind speed varying from 9kt to 13kt over the next three days.
